workContext.summary: User works at Jeeves Financial Technology (jeeves-org.slack.com) in underwriting (UW) operations, reviewing credit line decisions, onboarding scores, and account statuses. Their work involves tools such as Taktile (onboarding scoring), Redshift (data warehouse), Metabase (internal BI/SQL queries), python-pptx, and internal UW workflows including build_us.py, build_mx.py, merge_template.py, and data_tape.sql for borrowing base construction. They are involved with structured credit products including receivables-backed revolving facilities and line-of-credit facilities for business clients, and engage with external capital partners including Accial Capital, Lendable, CBIZ, CIM, Neuberger, Fasanara, Gramercy, Covalto, BBVA, and Vista Credit Partners.

personalContext.summary: User tends to favor pragmatic, lightweight solutions over over-engineered ones. They prefer simpler no-code or low-code alternatives where appropriate, are skeptical of self-hosted tooling, and prefer Reply All as the default behavior when drafting email responses. User expects the AI to remember established context without re-asking for clarification. User is privacy-conscious and receptive to honest pushback from the AI. User actively monitors stakeholder accountability and enforces exact structural alignment between trackers and folder structures. User is interested in AI tooling adoption across their team and is planning recurring internal knowledge-share sessions on AI tools including Claude Code. User enforces strict file hygiene in shared Drive folders — old/superseded versions of files must be deleted when a new version is dropped. User enforces source-citation discipline on all diligence deliverables — no claim may appear in a document without a verified source. User enforces precise language in diligence sitreps — documents must reflect current status only, with no forward-looking 'being prepared' language, and must distinguish between what a counterparty already has access to vs. what is available but not yet shared. User requires verbatim source questions in response documents rather than AI-summarized versions. User maintains a living `jeeves-diligence` skill that encodes all learned rules from BBVA, Vista, and future diligence processes — this skill is updated as new patterns are discovered. User requires all Drive deliverables to be Google Docs (not .md or other formats) unless a specific format is requested. User demands proof of data integrity when AI claims data ties to source — they will challenge the AI to verify and demonstrate exact matches. User expects the AI to actually open and inspect files before claiming it cannot verify them — failing to open a file before declaring it unreadable is considered an error. The correct reference for Jeeves investor presentation format is the 2026.02 Company Overview Google Slides deck — the Macropay PPTX is a single-slide balance chart and must never be used as a format reference for investor or diligence presentations. User requires a persistent skill be created that encodes the exact Jeeves slide format so every future deck is built to that spec without needing to re-specify it. The Jeeves slide format skill has been created at /mnt/skills/custom/jeeves-slides/SKILL.md and is now live — all future decks must load this skill first.

topOfMind.summary: User is actively engaged in BBVA due diligence, which escalated on April 20 when BBVA sent an updated tracker (David García, 1:13 AM email) with 40 items total: 22 complete, 9 in progress, 9 outstanding. Key new development: BBVA added a brand new Section 8 item (8.01) — PA Master flow explanation with avg inflows/outflows for last 6 months, marked prioritized and outstanding. The updated tracker was downloaded and uploaded to the BBVA DD folder in Drive; a tracker summary Google Doc was also created (Due Diligence - BBVA Tracker Summary - 20260420). User is also actively engaged in CIM amendment negotiations with Alexander Melikian, focused on finalizing commercial terms including max credit limit, concentration limits, hedging requirements for Colombia and Brazil assets, and Mastercard liability ramp. A new Vista Credit Partners due diligence process is ongoing (4/24 IC deadline). User has set up a weekly recurring 'Agent Office Hours' meeting starting Tuesday April 21, 1:00–2:00 PM PT, focused on AI tool knowledge sharing. A Neuberger Berman diligence call is scheduled for Thursday (week of April 20); Jay at NB sent an agenda and a slide deck shell was prepared to address the agenda points, saved to Debt/Neuberger Berman/Diligence/. The NB deck was initially built using the wrong format reference (Macropay PPTX) and had to be redone to match the actual 2026.02 Company Overview Google Slides deck format (near-black background #08080A, Urbanist font, gold #C7B06C accents, widescreen 10"×5.625"). A persistent Jeeves slide format skill has been created at /mnt/skills/custom/jeeves-slides/SKILL.md encoding the full format spec with explicit guardrails against using Calibri, navy blue, or the Macropay PPTX as a reference — this skill is now live and must be loaded for all future deck builds. The Ever DQ chart definition remains unresolved — reverse-engineered query produced wrong numbers (82% vs. expected 62% for H1 2021 at MOB 36). User ran the MX SOFOM Borrowing Base for April 19 on April 20, 2026: total SOFOM balance $65,200,884.56, eligible balance $58,679,490.31, tape 732 rows (Apr 17–19 range), uploaded to Debt/CIM/202604/MX/.

recentMonths.summary: User has been engaged in underwriting operations work at Jeeves, reviewing credit decisions and onboarding scores using Taktile and Redshift. They have been working with structured credit product documentation, monitoring credit exposure across their portfolio including Macropay in Mexico, and completed a polished PowerPoint deliverable for Macropay using python-pptx. User is engaged in capital markets term sheet negotiations with multiple external lenders: Fasanara (Timos Kouzelis) sent indicative terms on Mar 27, 2026, which user compared against Neuberger's fully negotiated and accepted counter-terms as a benchmark; on April 2, 2026, Fasanara sent updated terms (v2) via email, which were saved to Debt/Fasanara/ and redlined against the Jeeves comments version. User then produced a new Jeeves Comments turn on the Fasanara v2 terms with tracked-change pushbacks focused exclusively on commercial/fee terms. User completed the Gramercy term sheet redline task from Brian Mauck on April 1, 2026, and the Covalto Jeeves Comments turn (Term Sheet - Covalto-Jeeves - Jeeves Comments - 20260404.docx) — the Covalto item is now fully closed. User built a portfolio-level delinquency bucket breakdown chart, completed the Brian Mauck DQ30-59 DPD slide deliverable, and ran the MX SOFOM Borrowing Base daily using build_mx.py for March 27–31, 2026. On April 3 at approximately 20:45 PM, user completed a Colombia billing cycle migration analysis. On April 3, user also produced a Virtual Data Room facility overview summary document and completed the 2026 Credit Team OKRs comment workflow. On April 15, 2026, user investigated a post-charge-off account anomaly (Company 5937, Miss Tariffa, Italy). Also on April 15, user ran both the US Bridge Borrowing Base and MX SOFOM Borrowing Base for the April 14 reporting date. User completed a full rebuild of portfolio DQ and loss charts on April 15, producing three separate vintage DQ charts combined into a single 10-slide combined deck (5 global + 5 LATAM). On April 15–16, user conducted an intensive BBVA due diligence document collection and tracker update session. On April 16, Sateesh's tech infrastructure document was received and converted from HTML to PDF. Thiago submitted a large batch of BBVA diligence documents closing items 2.04, 6.02, 7.03, and 7.04. The BBVA Drive folder structure was reorganized into a new 'Due Diligence' parent folder reconciled to exactly 21 folders. On April 17, 2026, a new Vista Credit Partners due diligence process was initiated with a 4/24 IC deadline. On April 17 at 18:42, user set up a weekly recurring 'Agent Office Hours' meeting starting Tuesday April 21, 1:00–2:00 PM PT. On April 18, user explicitly delegated the task of sending the email to Melissa (MSchwabWright@goodwinlaw.com at Goodwin Law) about NB legal kickoff times to the AI. On April 20, 2026, BBVA sent an updated diligence tracker with 40 items total including a brand new Section 8 item (8.01) for PA Master flow explanation. Also on April 20, user challenged the AI to prove the DQ vintage deck raw data ties exactly to the charts — the AI verified 290/290 cohort × MOB combinations matched with zero mismatches. Also on April 20, Jay at Neuberger Berman sent a due diligence call agenda for a Thursday session; a Diligence subfolder was created under Debt/Neuberger Berman/ and a deck shell was prepared. During the NB deck session, the AI incorrectly used the Macropay PPTX as a format reference instead of the 2026.02 Company Overview Google Slides deck — user flagged this as an error and the deck was redone to match the correct format (near-black background #08080A, Urbanist font, gold #C7B06C accents, widescreen 10"×5.625"). The Macropay file is a single-slide balance chart and is explicitly not representative of the Jeeves investor presentation format. User subsequently requested a persistent skill be created encoding the Jeeves slide format spec — this skill was created at /mnt/skills/custom/jeeves-slides/SKILL.md with explicit guardrails against using Calibri, navy blue, light backgrounds, or the Macropay PPTX as a reference, and is now live for all future deck builds. On April 20, 2026 at 11:27 AM, user ran the MX SOFOM Borrowing Base for the April 19 reporting date: total SOFOM balance $65,200,884.56, eligible balance $58,679,490.31, tape 732 rows (Apr 17–19 range), file saved to Debt/CIM/202604/MX/.
